query:
You are working on the pinterest/ktlint repository.
The codebase is at version 0.49 (commit: 12b6eda3).

## Issue to Resolve

A type with multiple annotations inside a TypeArgumentList is not formatted correctly
Code below:
```
data class FooRequestDto(
    val data: List<@Valid @NotNull FooDto>,
)
```

is formatted as:
```
data class FooRequestDto(
    val data: List<@Valid @NotNull
    FooDto,>,
)
```

KtLint: 0.47.1

_Originally posted by @Vadgyik in https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/673#issuecomment-1351480443_

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

When I really exaggerate the example code, it turns out that it is not so easy to find an acceptable outcome that is deterministic when formatting generic type parameters that are annotated.

Suppose I have following classes and annotations:
```
@Target(AnnotationTarget.TYPE)
annotation class Foo
@Target(AnnotationTarget.TYPE)
annotation class Bar1
@Target(AnnotationTarget.TYPE)
annotation class Bar2
@Target(AnnotationTarget.TYPE)
annotation class FooBarLongDescription
@Target(AnnotationTarget.TYPE)
annotation class ComplexAnnotationWithParameters(val string: String)
class SimpleDto
class LongerButReasonableDto
class ExtremeLooooooooooooooooooooooooooooongDto
class SomeCustomTypeWithGenerics<A, B, C>
```

Example A below shows the code in which annotations are not wrapped:
```
data class ExampleA(
    val atom: List<@Foo SimpleDto>,
    val simpleList: List<@Foo @FooBarLongDescription @Bar1 @Bar2 SimpleDto>,
    val moreComplexxxxxxxxxxxxMap: Map<@Foo @FooBarLongDescription @Bar1 @Bar2 SimpleDto, @Foo @FooBarLongDescription LongerButReasonableDto>,
    val veryLoooooooooooooooooooooooooongCustomType: SomeCustomTypeWithGenerics<@Foo @FooBarLongDescription @Bar1 @Bar2 SimpleDto, @Foo @FooBarLongDescription LongerButReasonableDto, @Suppress("AnnotationRule") @Foo @FooBarLongDescription ExtremeLooooooooooooooooooooooooooooongDto>,
)
```

This can be made more readable by wrapping each generic type having multiple annotations:
```
data class ExampleB(
    val atom: List<@Foo SimpleDto>,
    val simpleList:
        List<
            @Foo @FooBarLongDescription @Bar1 @Bar2 SimpleDto,
        >,
    val moreComplexxxxxxxxxxxxMap: Map<
            @Foo @FooBarLongDescription @Bar1 @Bar2 SimpleDto,
            @Foo @FooBarLongDescription LongerButReasonableDto,
        >,
    val veryLoooooooooooooooooooooooooongCustomType:
        SomeCustomTypeWithGenerics<
            @Foo @FooBarLongDescription @Bar1 @Bar2 SimpleDto,
            @Foo @FooBarLongDescription LongerButReasonableDto,
            @ComplexAnnotationWithParameters("foo bar foo bar foo bar foo bar foo bar foo bar foo bar") @Foo @FooBarLongDescription ExtremeLooooooooooooooooooooooooooooongDto,
        >,
)
```
A valid question with the example above is whether the same should also have be done with `atom: List<@Foo SimpleDto>`. The biggest downside of this approach is that formatting is not consistent with the way that annotations are formatted on normal parameters or classes.

If each annotation is wrapped to a separate line, the result would look like:
```
data class ExampleC(
    val atom: List<@Foo SimpleDto>,
    val simpleList: List<
        @Foo
        @FooBarLongDescription
        @Bar1
        @Bar2
        SimpleDto
    >,
    val moreComplexxxxxxxxxxxxMap: Map<
        @Foo
        @FooBarLongDescription
        @Bar1
        @Bar2
        SimpleDto,
        @Foo
        @FooBarLongDescription
        LongerButReasonableDto
    >,
    val veryLoooooooooooooooooooooooooongCustomType: SomeCustomTypeWithGenerics<
        @Foo
        @FooBarLongDescription
        @Bar1
        @Bar2
        SimpleDto,
        @Foo
        @FooBarLongDescription
        LongerButReasonableDto,
        @ComplexAnnotationWithParameters("foo bar foo bar foo bar foo bar foo bar foo bar foo bar")
        @Foo
        @FooBarLongDescription
        ExtremeLooooooooooooooooooooooooooooongDto,
    >,
)
```
Advantage is that this is consistent with other formatting. Disadvantage of course is that it is lengthy. Despite that, I am inclined to choose this formatting.

But when combined with the rule suppression mechanism, the developer could choose in specific case to use a manual formatting like below:
```
@Suppress("ktlint:annotation")
data class ExampleD(
    val atom: List<@Foo SimpleDto>,
    val simpleList: List<@Foo @FooBarLongDescription SimpleDto>,
    val moreComplexxxxxxxxxxxxMap:
        Map<
            @Foo @FooBarLongDescription
            SimpleDto,
            @Foo @FooBarLongDescription
            LongerButReasonableDto
        >,
    val veryLoooooooooooooooooooooooooongCustomType:
        SomeCustomTypeWithGenerics<
            @Foo @FooBarLongDescription SimpleDto,
            @Foo @FooBarLongDescription
            LongerButReasonableDto,
            @ComplexAnnotationWithParameters("foo bar foo bar foo bar foo bar foo bar foo bar foo bar")
            @Foo
            @FooBarLongDescription
            ExtremeLooooooooooooooooooooooooooooongDto,
        >,
)
```

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `docs/faq.md`
- `ktlint-rule-engine-core/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/rule/engine/core/api/ASTNodeExtension.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/AnnotationRule.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/ArgumentListWrappingRule.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/BlockCommentInitialStarAlignmentRule.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/ChainWrappingRule.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/CommentWrappingRule.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/ContextReceiverWrappingRule.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/FunctionSignatureRule.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/IfElseBracingRule.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/KdocWrappingRule.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/ModifierListSpacingRule.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/MultiLineIfElseRule.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/NoEmptyFirstLineInClassBodyRule.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/ParameterListWrappingRule.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/ParameterWrappingRule.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/PropertyWrappingRule.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/TrailingCommaOnDeclarationSiteRule.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/WrappingRule.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

 26→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 27→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.PsiWhiteSpace
 28→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.LeafPsiElement
 29→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.PsiWhiteSpaceImpl
 30→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.descriptors.annotations.AnnotationUseSiteTarget
 31→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtAnnotationEntry
 32→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.psiUtil.leaves
 33→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.psiUtil.siblings
 34→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.utils.addToStdlib.safeAs
 35→
 36→/**
 37→ * Ensures multiple annotations are not on the same line as the annotated declaration. Also ensures that annotations
 38→ * with parameters are placed on separate lines.
 39→ *
 40→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#annotation-formatting
 41→ *
 42→ * @see [AnnotationSpacingRule] for white space rules. Moved since
 43→ */
 44→public class AnnotationRule : StandardRule("annotation") {
 45→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 46→        node: ASTNode,
 47→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 48→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 49→    ) {
 50→        when (node.elementType) {
 51→            FILE_ANNOTATION_LIST -> {
 52→                visitFileAnnotationList(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 53→            }
 54→            ANNOTATION -> {
 55→                visitAnnotation(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 56→            }
 57→            ANNOTATION_ENTRY ->
 58→                visitAnnotationEntry(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 59→        }
 60→    }
 61→
 62→    private fun visitAnnotationEntry(
 63→        node: ASTNode,
 64→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 65→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 66→    ) {
 67→        require(node.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY)
 68→
 69→        if (node.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() &&
 70→            node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_PARAMETER && // fun fn(@Ann("blah") a: String)
 71→            node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_ARGUMENT && // fn(@Ann("blah") "42")
 72→            !node.isPartOf(TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST) && // val property: Map<@Ann("blah") String, Int>
 73→            node.isNotReceiverTargetAnnotation()
 74→        ) {
 75→            checkForAnnotationWithParameterToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 76→        }
 77→
 78→        if ((node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry()) ||
 79→            (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct())
 80→        ) {
 81→            checkForAnnotationToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 82→        }
 83→
 84→        if (node.treeParent.elementType != ANNOTATION &&
 85→            node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() &&
 86→            node.isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct()
 87→        ) {
 88→            checkForMultipleAnnotationsOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 89→        }
 90→    }
 91→
 92→    private fun checkForAnnotationWithParameterToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(
 93→        node: ASTNode,
 94→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 95→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 96→    ) {
 97→        if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry()) {
 98→            emit(
 99→                node.startOffset,
100→                "Annotation with parameter(s) should be placed on a separate line prior to the annotated construct",
101→                true,
102→            )
103→            if (autoCorrect) {
104→                node
105→                    .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
106→                    .upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(" ")
107→            }
108→        }
109→
110→        if (node.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntryOrAnnotatedConstruct()) {
111→            emit(
112→                node.startOffset,
113→                "Annotation with parameter(s) should be placed on a separate line prior to the annotated construct",
114→                // Annotated expressions for which the annotation contains a parameter can be hard to correct
115→                // automatically. See examples below. For now, let them be fixed manually.
116→                // fun foo1() = @Suppress("DEPRECATION") bar()
117→                // if (@Suppress("DEPRECATION") bar()) { .. }
118→                node.treeParent.elementType != ANNOTATED_EXPRESSION,
119→            )
120→            if (autoCorrect) {
121→                node
122→                    .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
123→                    .nextLeaf()
124→                    .safeAs<LeafPsiElement>()
125→                    ?.let {
126→                        if (it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE) {
127→                            it.replaceWithText(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
128→                        } else {
129→                            it.rawInsertBeforeMe(
130→                                PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent)),
131→                            )
132→                        }
133→                    }
134→            }
135→        }
136→    }
137→
138→    private fun checkForMultipleAnnotationsOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct(
139→        node: ASTNode,
140→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
141→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
142→    ) {
143→        if (node.isLastAnnotationEntry()) {
144→            val noAnnotationWithParameters =
145→                node
146→                    .siblings(forward = false)
147→                    .none { it.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() }
148→            if (noAnnotationWithParameters) {
149→                emit(
150→                    node.treeParent.startOffset,
151→                    "Multiple annotations should not be placed on the same line as the annotated construct",
152→                    true,
153→                )
154→                if (autoCorrect) {
155→                    node
156→                        .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
157→                        .nextCodeLeaf()
158→                        ?.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
159→                }
160→            }
161→        }
162→    }
163→
164→    private fun checkForAnnotationToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(
165→        node: ASTNode,
166→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
167→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
168→    ) {
169→        val isFollowedWithAnnotationHavingValueArgumentList =
170→            node
171→                .siblings(forward = true)
172→                .any { it.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() }
173→        val isPrecededWithAnnotationOnOtherLine =
174→            node
175→                .siblings(forward = false)
176→                .any { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
177→        if (isFollowedWithAnnotationHavingValueArgumentList || isPrecededWithAnnotationOnOtherLine) {
178→            emit(
179→                node.startOffset,
180→                "Annotation must be placed on separate line",
181→                true,
182→            )
183→            if (autoCorrect) {
184→                node
185→                    .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
186→                    .nextLeaf()
187→                    ?.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
188→            }
189→        }
190→    }
191→
192→    private fun ASTNode.isNotReceiverTargetAnnotation() = getAnnotationUseSiteTarget() != AnnotationUseSiteTarget.RECEIVER
193→
194→    private fun ASTNode.getAnnotationUseSiteTarget() =
195→        psi
196→            .safeAs<KtAnnotationEntry>()
197→            ?.useSiteTarget
198→            ?.getAnnotationUseSiteTarget()
199→
200→    private fun ASTNode.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() = getAnnotationEntryValueArgumentList() != null
201→
202→    private fun ASTNode.getAnnotationEntryValueArgumentList() =
203→        takeIf { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
204→            ?.findChildByType(VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST)
205→
206→    private fun ASTNode.isLastAnnotationEntry() =
207→        treeParent
208→            .children()
209→            .lastOrNull { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
210→            .let { it == this }
211→
212→    private fun ASTNode.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() = siblings(forward = false).any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
213→
214→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry() =
215→        siblings(forward = false)
216→            .takeWhile { it.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
217→            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
218→
219→    private fun ASTNode.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry() = siblings(forward = true).any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
220→
221→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry() =
222→        siblings(forward = true)
223→            .takeWhile { it.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
224→            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
225→
226→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct() =
227→        lastChildLeafOrSelf()
228→            .leaves(forward = true)
229→            .takeWhile { it.isWhiteSpace() || it.isPartOfComment() }
230→            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
231→
232→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntryOrAnnotatedConstruct() =
233→        if (isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
234→            isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry()
235→        } else {
236→            isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct()
237→        }
238→
239→    private fun visitFileAnnotationList(
240→        node: ASTNode,
241→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
242→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
243→    ) {
244→        node
245→            .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
246→            .nextCodeLeaf()
247→            ?.let { codeLeaf ->
248→                val whitespaceBefore = codeLeaf.prevLeaf { it.isWhiteSpace() }
249→
250→                if (whitespaceBefore == null || whitespaceBefore.text != "\n\n") {
251→                    emit(
252→                        codeLeaf.startOffset,
253→                        "File annotations should be separated from file contents with a blank line",
254→                        true,
255→                    )
256→                    if (autoCorrect) {
257→                        codeLeaf.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe("\n\n")
258→                    }
259→                }
260→            }
261→    }
262→
263→    private fun visitAnnotation(
264→        node: ASTNode,
265→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
266→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
267→    ) {
268→        require(node.elementType == ANNOTATION)
269→
270→        if ((node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry()) ||
271→            (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry())
272→        ) {
273→            emit(
274→                node.startOffset,
275→                "@[...] style annotations should be on a separate line from other annotations.",
276→                true,
277→            )
278→            if (autoCorrect) {
279→                if (node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
280→                    node.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
281→                } else if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
282→                    node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
283→                }
284→            }
285→        }
286→    }
287→
288→    private fun getNewlineWithIndent(modifierListRoot: ASTNode): String {
289→        val nodeBeforeAnnotations = modifierListRoot.treeParent.treePrev as? PsiWhiteSpace
290→        // If there is no whitespace before the annotation, the annotation is the first
291→        // text in the file
292→        val newLineWithIndent = nodeBeforeAnnotations?.text ?: "\n"
293→        return if (newLineWithIndent.contains('\n')) {
294→            // Make sure we only insert a single newline
295→            newLineWithIndent.substring(newLineWithIndent.lastIndexOf('\n'))
296→        } else {
297→            newLineWithIndent
298→        }
299→    }
300→}
301→
302→public val ANNOTATION_RULE_ID: RuleId = AnnotationRule().ruleId
303→

 7→class TypeArgumentListSpacingRuleTest {
 8→    private val typeArgumentListSpacingRuleAssertThat = assertThatRule { TypeArgumentListSpacingRule() }
 9→
10→    @Test

19→            """.trimIndent()
20→        typeArgumentListSpacingRuleAssertThat(code)
21→            .hasLintViolations(

24→                LintViolation(1, 32, "No whitespace expected at this position"),

26→            ).isFormattedAs(formattedCode)

35→            }

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules
  2→
  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType
  4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.ELSE

  6→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.IndentConfig
  7→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule.VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule.Mode.REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED
  8→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
  9→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.column
 10→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfig
 11→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY
 12→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY
 13→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY
 14→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isPartOfComment
 15→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isWhiteSpace
 16→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline
 17→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.lineIndent
 18→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.prevLeaf
 19→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
 20→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 21→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.PsiWhiteSpace
 22→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.LeafPsiElement
 23→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.PsiWhiteSpaceImpl
 24→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtContainerNode
 25→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtDoWhileExpression
 26→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtIfExpression
 27→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtWhileExpression
 28→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.psiUtil.children
 29→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.psiUtil.getStrictParentOfType
 30→
 31→/**
 32→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#method-call-formatting
 33→ *
 34→ * The rule is more aggressive in inserting newlines after arguments than mentioned in the styleguide:
 35→ * Each argument should be on a separate line if
 36→ * - at least one of the arguments is
 37→ * - maxLineLength exceeded (and separating arguments with \n would actually help)
 38→ * in addition, "(" and ")" must be on separates line if any of the arguments are (otherwise on the same)
 39→ */
 40→public class ArgumentListWrappingRule :
 41→    StandardRule(
 42→        id = "argument-list-wrapping",
 43→        visitorModifiers = setOf(
 44→            VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule(
 45→                // ArgumentListWrapping should only be used in case after normal wrapping the max_line_length is still
 46→                // violated
 47→                ruleId = WRAPPING_RULE_ID,
 48→                mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
 49→            ),
 50→        ),
 51→        usesEditorConfigProperties = setOf(
 52→            INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 53→            INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 54→            MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
 55→        ),
 56→    ) {
 57→    private var editorConfigIndent = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 58→
 59→    private var maxLineLength = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 60→
 61→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 62→        editorConfigIndent = IndentConfig(
 63→            indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 64→            tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 65→        )
 66→        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
 67→    }
 68→
 69→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 70→        node: ASTNode,
 71→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 72→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 73→    ) {
 74→        if (editorConfigIndent.disabled) {
 75→            return
 76→        }
 77→
 78→        if (node.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST) {
 79→            if (needToWrapArgumentList(node)) {
 80→                val newIndentLevel = node.getNewIndentLevel()
 81→                node
 82→                    .children()
 83→                    .forEach { child -> wrapArgumentInList(newIndentLevel, child, emit, autoCorrect) }
 84→            }
 85→        }
 86→    }
 87→
 88→    private fun needToWrapArgumentList(node: ASTNode) =
 89→        if ( // skip when there are no arguments
 90→            node.firstChildNode?.treeNext?.elementType != ElementType.RPAR &&
 91→            // skip lambda arguments
 92→            node.treeParent?.elementType != ElementType.FUNCTION_LITERAL &&
 93→            // skip if number of arguments is big (we assume it with a magic number of 8)
 94→            node.children().count { it.elementType == ElementType.VALUE_ARGUMENT } <= 8
 95→        ) {
 96→            // each argument should be on a separate line if
 97→            // - at least one of the arguments is
 98→            // - maxLineLength exceeded (and separating arguments with \n would actually help)
 99→            // in addition, "(" and ")" must be on separates line if any of the arguments are (otherwise on the same)
100→            node.textContainsIgnoringLambda('\n') || node.exceedsMaxLineLength()
101→        } else {
102→            false
103→        }
104→
105→    private fun ASTNode.exceedsMaxLineLength() = (column - 1 + textLength) > maxLineLength && !textContains('\n')
106→
107→    private fun ASTNode.getNewIndentLevel(): Int {
108→        val currentIndentLevel = editorConfigIndent.indentLevelFrom(lineIndent())
109→        return when {
110→            // IDEA quirk:
111→            // generic<
112→            //     T,
113→            //     R>(
114→            //     1,
115→            //     2
116→            // )
117→            // instead of
118→            // generic<
119→            //     T,
120→            //     R>(
121→            //         1,
122→            //         2
123→            //     )
124→            currentIndentLevel > 0 && hasTypeArgumentListInFront() -> currentIndentLevel - 1
125→
126→            // IDEA quirk:
127→            // foo
128→            //     .bar = Baz(
129→            //     1,
130→            //     2
131→            // )
132→            // instead of
133→            // foo
134→            //     .bar = Baz(
135→            //         1,
136→            //         2
137→            //     )
138→            currentIndentLevel > 0 && isPartOfDotQualifiedAssignmentExpression() -> currentIndentLevel - 1
139→
140→            else -> currentIndentLevel
141→        }.let {
142→            if (isOnSameLineAsControlFlowKeyword()) {
143→                it + 1
144→            } else {
145→                it
146→            }
147→        }
148→    }
149→
150→    private fun wrapArgumentInList(
151→        newIndentLevel: Int,
152→        child: ASTNode,
153→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
154→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
155→    ) {
156→        val indent = "\n" + editorConfigIndent.indent.repeat(newIndentLevel)
157→        when (child.elementType) {
158→            ElementType.LPAR -> {
159→                val prevLeaf = child.prevLeaf()
160→                if (prevLeaf is PsiWhiteSpace && prevLeaf.textContains('\n')) {
161→                    emit(child.startOffset, errorMessage(child), true)
162→                    if (autoCorrect) {
163→                        prevLeaf.delete()
164→                    }
165→                }
166→            }
167→            ElementType.VALUE_ARGUMENT,
168→            ElementType.RPAR,
169→            -> {
170→                // aiming for
171→                // ... LPAR
172→                // <line indent + indentSize> VALUE_PARAMETER...
173→                // <line indent> RPAR
174→                val intendedIndent = if (child.elementType == ElementType.VALUE_ARGUMENT) {
175→                    indent + editorConfigIndent.indent
176→                } else {
177→                    indent
178→                }
179→
180→                val prevLeaf = child.prevWhiteSpaceWithNewLine() ?: child.prevLeaf()
181→                if (prevLeaf is PsiWhiteSpace) {
182→                    if (prevLeaf.getText().contains("\n")) {
183→                        // The current child is already wrapped to a new line. Checking and fixing the
184→                        // correct size of the indent is the responsibility of the IndentationRule.
185→                        return
186→                    } else {
187→                        // The current child needs to be wrapped to a newline.
188→                        emit(child.startOffset, errorMessage(child), true)
189→                        if (autoCorrect) {
190→                            // The indentation is purely based on the previous leaf only. Note that in
191→                            // autoCorrect mode the indent rule, if enabled, runs after this rule and
192→                            // determines the final indentation. But if the indent rule is disabled then the
193→                            // indent of this rule is kept.
194→                            (prevLeaf as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(intendedIndent)
195→                        }
196→                    }
197→                } else {
198→                    // Insert a new whitespace element in order to wrap the current child to a new line.
199→                    emit(child.startOffset, errorMessage(child), true)
200→                    if (autoCorrect) {
201→                        child.treeParent.addChild(PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(intendedIndent), child)
202→                    }
203→                }
204→                // Indentation of child nodes need to be fixed by the IndentationRule.
205→            }
206→        }
207→    }
208→
209→    private fun errorMessage(node: ASTNode) =
210→        when (node.elementType) {
211→            ElementType.LPAR -> """Unnecessary newline before "(""""
212→            ElementType.VALUE_ARGUMENT ->
213→                "Argument should be on a separate line (unless all arguments can fit a single line)"
214→            ElementType.RPAR -> """Missing newline before ")""""
215→            else -> throw UnsupportedOperationException()
216→        }
217→
218→    private fun ASTNode.textContainsIgnoringLambda(char: Char): Boolean {
219→        return children().any { child ->
220→            val elementType = child.elementType
221→            elementType == ElementType.WHITE_SPACE && child.textContains(char) ||
222→                elementType == ElementType.COLLECTION_LITERAL_EXPRESSION && child.textContains(char) ||
223→                elementType == ElementType.VALUE_ARGUMENT && child.children().any { it.textContainsIgnoringLambda(char) }
224→        }
225→    }
226→
227→    private fun ASTNode.hasTypeArgumentListInFront(): Boolean =
228→        treeParent.children()
229→            .firstOrNull { it.elementType == ElementType.TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST }
230→            ?.children()
231→            ?.any { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() } == true
232→
233→    private fun ASTNode.isPartOfDotQualifiedAssignmentExpression(): Boolean =
234→        treeParent?.treeParent?.elementType == ElementType.BINARY_EXPRESSION &&
235→            treeParent?.treeParent?.children()?.find { it.elementType == ElementType.DOT_QUALIFIED_EXPRESSION } != null
236→
237→    private fun ASTNode.prevWhiteSpaceWithNewLine(): ASTNode? {
238→        var prev = prevLeaf()
239→        while (prev != null && (prev.isWhiteSpace() || prev.isPartOfComment())) {
240→            if (prev.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
241→                return prev
242→            }
243→            prev = prev.prevLeaf()
244→        }
245→        return null
246→    }
247→
248→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsControlFlowKeyword(): Boolean {
249→        val containerNode = psi.getStrictParentOfType<KtContainerNode>() ?: return false
250→        if (containerNode.node.elementType == ELSE) return false
251→        val controlFlowKeyword = when (val parent = containerNode.parent) {
252→            is KtIfExpression -> parent.ifKeyword.node
253→            is KtWhileExpression -> parent.firstChild.node
254→            is KtDoWhileExpression -> parent.whileKeyword?.node
255→            else -> null
256→        } ?: return false
257→
258→        var prevLeaf = prevLeaf() ?: return false
259→        while (prevLeaf != controlFlowKeyword) {
260→            if (prevLeaf.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) return false
261→            prevLeaf = prevLeaf.prevLeaf() ?: return false
262→        }
263→        return true
264→    }
265→}
266→
267→public val ARGUMENT_LIST_WRAPPING_RULE_ID: RuleId = ArgumentListWrappingRule().ruleId
268→

conversation_history.name: read
conversation_history.content:
1→# Changelog
  2→All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
  3→This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
  4→
  5→## Unreleased
  6→
  7→WARNING: This version on KtLint contains a number of breaking changes in KtLint CLI and KtLint API. If you are using KtLint with custom ruleset jars or custom reporter jars, then those need to be upgrade before you can use them with this version of ktlint. Please contact the maintainers of those jars and ask them to upgrade a.s.a.p.
  8→
  9→All rule id's in the output of Ktlint are now prefixed with a rule set. Although KtLint currently supports standard rules to be unqualified, users are encouraged to migrate to include the rule set id in all references to rules. This includes following:
 10→- The `--disabled-rules` parameter in KtLint CLI and `.editorconfig`.
 11→- The `source` element in the KtLint CLI `baseline.xml` file. Regenerating this file, fixes all rule references automatically.
 12→- The KtLint disable directives `ktlint-enable` / `ktlint-disable` and the `@Suppress('ktlint:...')` annotations.
 13→
 14→### Moving experimental rules to standard rule set
 15→
 16→The `experimental` rule set has been merged with `standard` rule set. The rules which formerly were part of the `experimental` rule set are still being treated as before. The rules will only be run in case `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled or in case the rule is explicitly enabled.
 17→
 18→Note that the prefix `experimental:` has to be removed from all references to this rule. Check references in:
 19→* The `.editorconfig` setting `disabled_rules`.
 20→* KtLint disable and enable directives.
 21→* The `VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule`.
 22→
 23→### API Changes & RuleSet providers & Reporter Providers
 24→
 25→#### Experimental rules
 26→
 27→Rules in custom rule sets can be marked as experimental by implementing the `Rule.Experimental` interface on the rule. Rules marked with this interface will only be executed by Ktlint whenever the `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled.
 28→
 29→When using this feature, experimental rules should *not* be defined in a separate rule set. A rule is identified by its qualified rule id that includes the rule set id. Moving a rule from an experimental rule set to a non-experimental rule set has the downside that the qualified rule id changes. For users of such rules this means that ktlint directives to suppress the rule and properties in the `.editorconfig` files have to be changed.
 30→
 31→#### EditorConfig
 32→
 33→Field `defaultAndroidValue` in class `EditorConfigProperty` has been renamed to `androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue`. Note that also new fields `ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue` and `intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue` have been added. Read more about this in the section "Ktlint Official code style".
 34→
 35→The `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` are no longer supported. Specifying those properties in the `editorConfigOverride` or `editorConfigDefaults` result in warnings at runtime.
 36→
 37→#### 'Ktlint Official` code style and renaming of existing code styles
 38→
 39→A new code style with name `ktlint_offical` has been added. This code style makes it possible to innovate ktlint beyond current expectations of users that are familiar with the existing code styles. 
 40→
 41→The `ktlint_official` code style combines the best elements from the Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This codestyle also provides additional formatting on topics which are not (explicitly) mentioned in the before mentioned styleguide. Also, this code style sometimes formats code in a way which is not accepted by the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. The formatters of those editors produce nicely formatted code in the vast majority of cases. But in a number of edge cases, the formatting contains bugs which are waiting to be fixed for several years. The new code style formats code in a way which is compatible with the default formatting of the editors whenever possible. When using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) code formatting in the editor. In the long run, this code style becomes the default code style provided by KtLint.
 42→
 43→The `official` code style has been renamed to `intellij_idea`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of IntelliJ IDEA. This code style is based on Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to then do not forget to change the value of that property to `intellij_idea`. When not set, this is still the default code style of ktlint. Note that in the long run, the default code style is changed to `ktlint_official`.
 44→
 45→Code style `android` has been renamed to `android_studio`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of Android Studio. This code style is based on Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to then do not forget to change the value of that property to `android_studio`.
 46→
 47→#### Package restructuring and class relocation
 48→
 49→The internal structure of the Ktlint project has been revised. The Ktlint CLI and KtLint API modules have been decoupled where possible. Modules have been restructured and renamed. See [API Overview](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/api/overview/) for more information.
 50→
 51→This is the last release that contains module `ktlint-core` as it had too many responsibilities. All classes in this module are relocated to other modules. Some classes have also been renamed. See tables below for details. Classes that are left behind in the `ktlint-core` module are deprecated and were kept in this version for backwards compatibility only. The `ktlint-core` module will be removed in Ktlint `0.50.x`.
 52→
 53→Classes below have been moved to the new module `ktlint-rule-engine`:
 54→
 55→| Old class/package name in `ktlint-core`                  | New class/package name in `ktlint-rule-engine-core`                               |
 56→|----------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
 57→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.editorconfig               | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig                            |
 58→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.EditorConfigProperties     | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.EditorConfig                            |
 59→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.OptInFeatures              | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.OptInFeatures                           |
 60→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.ElementType                | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType                             |
 61→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.package                    | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ASTNodeExtension                        |
 62→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.IndentConfig                   | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.IndentConfig                            |
 63→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.Rule                           | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule                                    |
 64→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.RuleProvider                   | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleProvider                            |

 73→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.KtLint                         | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.KtLintRuleEngine                        |
 74→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.LintError                      | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.LintError                               |
 75→
 76→Class `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.KtLint.Code.CodeFile` has been replaced with factory method `com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.Code.fromFile`. Likewise, class `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.KtLint.Code.CodeSnippet` has been replaced with factory method `com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.Code.fromSnippet`.
 77→
 78→Classes below have been moved to the new module `ktlint-cli-ruleset-core`:
 79→
 80→| Old class/package name in `ktlint-core`                  | New class/package name in `ktlint-cli-ruleset-core`                           |
 81→|----------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------|

 83→
 84→
 85→Class below has been moved to the new module `ktlint-cli-reporter-core`:
 86→
 87→| Old class/package name in `ktlint-core`   | New class/package name in `ktlint-cli-reporter-core`     |
 88→|-------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------|

 90→
 91→Class below has been moved to the new module `ktlint-logger`:
 92→
 93→| Old class/package name in `ktlint-core`               | New class/package name in `ktlint-logger`                   |
 94→|-------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------|

 96→
 97→Module `ktlint` has been renamed to `ktlint-cli`. Classes below have been relocated in this module:
 98→
 99→| Old class/package name in `ktlint-core` | New class/package name in `ktlint-cli` |
100→|-----------------------------------------|----------------------------------------|
101→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.Baseline  | com.pinterest.ktlint.cli.api.Baseline  |
102→
103→Module `ktlint-reporter-baseline` has been renamed to `ktlint-cli-reporter-baseline`. Classes below have been relocated:
104→
105→| Old class/package name in `ktlint-reporter-baseline` | New class/package name in `ktlint-cli-reporter-baseline` |
106→|------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------|
107→| com.pinterest.ktlint.reporter.baseline               | com.pinterest.ktlint.cli.reporter.baseline               |

151→#### Custom Ruleset Provider `RuleSetProviderV2`
152→
153→Custom Rule Sets build for older versions of KtLint no longer supported by this version of KtLint. The `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.RuleSetProviderV2` interface has been replaced with `RuleSetProviderV3`. The accompanying interfaces `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.RuleProvider` and `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.Rule` have been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.core.api.RuleProvider` and `com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.core.api.Rule` respectively.
154→
155→Contrary to `RuleSetProviderV2`, the `RuleSetProviderV3` no longer contains information about the rule set. About information now has to be specified in the new `Rule` class. This allows custom rule set providers to combine rules originating from different rule sets into a new rule set without loosing information about its origin. The typ of the id of the rule set is changed from `String` to `RuleSetId`. 
156→
157→Note that due to renaming and relocation of the `RuleSetProviderV3` interface the name of the service provider in the custom reporter needs to be changed from `resources/META-INF/services/com.pinterest.ktlint.core.RuleSetProviderV2` to `resources/META-INF/services/com.pinterest.ktlint.cli.ruleset.core.api.RuleSetProviderV3`.
158→
159→The rule id's in `com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.core.api.Rule` have been changed from type `String` to `RuleId`. A `RuleId` has a value that must adhere the convention "`rule-set-id`:`rule-id`". The rule set id `standard` is reserved for rules which are maintained by the KtLint project. Rules created by custom rule set providers and API Consumers should use a prefix other than `standard` to mark the origin of rules which are not maintained by the KtLint project.
160→
161→When wrapping a rule from the ktlint project and modifying its behavior, please change the `ruleId` and `about` fields in the wrapped rule, so that it is clear to users whenever they use the original rule provided by KtLint versus a modified version which is not maintained by the KtLint project.
162→
163→The typealias `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.EditorConfigProperties` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.EditorConfig`. The interface `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.UsesEditorConfigProperties` has been removed. Instead, the Rule property `usesEditorConfigProperties` needs to be set. As a result of those changes, the `beforeFirstNode` function in each rule has to changed to something like below: 
164→
165→```kotlin
166→public class SomeRule : Rule(
167→  ruleId = RuleId("some-rule-set:some-rule"),
168→  usesEditorConfigProperties = setOf(MY_EDITOR_CONFIG_PROPERTY),
169→) {
170→  private lateinit var myEditorConfigProperty: MyEditorConfigProperty
171→
172→  override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
173→    myEditorConfigProperty = editorConfig[MY_EDITOR_CONFIG_PROPERTY]
174→  }
175→  
176→  ...
177→}
178→```
179→
180→Fields `loadOnlyWhenOtherRuleIsLoaded` and `runOnlyWhenOtherRuleIsEnabled` have been removed from class `com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule.VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule` and are replaced with a single field `mode`. The `mode` either contains value `REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED` or `ONLY_WHEN_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_AND_ENABLED`.
181→
182→#### Custom Reporter Provider `ReporterProvider`
183→
184→Custom Reporters build for older versions of KtLint are no longer supported by this version of KtLint. The `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ReporterProvider` interface has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.cli.reporter.core.api.ReporterProviderV2`. The accompanying interface `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.Reporter` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.cli.reporter.core.api.ReporterV2`.
185→
186→Note that due to renaming and relocation of the `ReporterProviderV2` interface the name of the service provider in the custom reporter needs to be changed from `resources/META-INF/services/com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ReporterProvider` to `resources/META-INF/services/com.pinterest.ktlint.cli.reporter.core.api.ReporterProviderV2`.
187→
188→The biggest change in the `ReporterV2` is the replacement of the `LintError` class with `KtlintCliError` class. The latter class now contains a status field which more clearly explains the difference between a lint error which can be autocorrected versus a lint error that actually has been autocorrected.
189→
190→#### Custom rules provided by API Consumer
191→
192→API Consumers provide a set of rules directly to the Ktlint Rule Engine. The `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.Rule` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.core.api.Rule`.
193→
194→The type of the rule id's has been changed from type `String` to `RuleId`. A `RuleId` has a value that must adhere the convention "`rule-set-id`:`rule-id`". Rule set id `standard` is reserved for rules which are maintained by the KtLint project. Custom rules created by the API Consumer should use a prefix other than `standard` to clearly mark the origin of rules which are not maintained by the KtLint project.
195→
196→Also, the field `About` has been added. This field specifies the name of the maintainer, and the repository url and issue tracker url of the rule.
197→
198→When wrapping a rule from the ktlint project and modifying its behavior, please change the `ruleId` and `about` fields in the wrapped rule, so that it is clear to users whenever they use the original rule provided by KtLint versus a modified version which is not maintained by the KtLint project.
199→
200→The typealias `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.EditorConfigProperties` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.EditorConfig`. The interface `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.UsesEditorConfigProperties` has been removed. Instead, the Rule property `usesEditorConfigProperties` needs to be set. 1040→Minor release to support projects using mixed 1.3/1.4 Kotlin versions (e.g. Gradle plugins)

1045→## [0.38.0] - 2020-08-21
1046→
1047→New release with Kotlin 1.4.0 support and several enhancements and bugfixes.
1048→
1049→### Added
1050→- Experimental SpacingAroundAngleBracketsRule ([#769](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/769))
1051→- Checksum generation for executable Jar ([#695](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/695))
1052→- Enable Gradle dependency verification
1053→- `parameter-list-wrapping` rule now also considers function arguments while wrapping ([#620](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/620))
1054→- Publish snapshots built against kotlin development versions
1055→- Initial support for tab-based indentation ([#128](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/128))
1056→
1057→### Fixed
1058→- Safe-called wrapped trailing lambdas indented correctly ([#776](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/776))
1059→- `provideDelegate` imports are not marked as unused anymore ([#669](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/669))
1060→- Set continuation indent to 4 in IDE integration codestyle ([#775](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/775)) 
1061→- No empty lines between annotation and annotated target ([#688](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/688))
1062→- Unused imports reported correctly ([#526](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/526)) ([#405](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/405))
1063→- No false empty lines inserted in multiline if-else block ([#793](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/793))
1064→- No-wildcard-imports properly handles custom infix function with asterisk ([#799](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/799))
1065→- Do not require else to be in the same line of a right brace if the right brace is not part of the if statement ([#756](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/756))
1066→- Brace-less if-else bodies starting with parens indented correctly ([#829](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/829))
1067→- If-condition with multiline call expression inside indented correctly ([#796](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/796))
1068→
1069→### Changed
1070→- Update Gradle to 6.6 version
1071→- Update ec4j to 0.2.2 version. Now it should report path to `.editorconfig` file on failed parsing 
1072→and allow empty `.editorconfig` files.
1073→- Update Kotlin to 1.4.0 version ([#830](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/830))
1074→
1075→

1078→Minor release to fix further bugs in `ImportOrderingRule`.

1089→- Imports with `as` no longer removed ([#766](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/766))
1090→- The contents of raw strings are no longer modified by the indent rule ([#682](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/682))
1091→
1092→## [0.37.0] - 2020-06-02
1093→
1094→Thank you to [Tapchicoma](https://github.com/Tapchicoma) and [romtsn](https://github.com/romtsn) for all their hard work on this release!

1100→- `kotlin_imports_layout` config for `.editorconfig` file so that import ordering is configurable. Fixes ([#527](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/527))
1101→
1102→

200→The typealias `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.EditorConfigProperties` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.EditorConfig`. The interface `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.UsesEditorConfigProperties` has been removed. Instead, the Rule property `usesEditorConfigProperties` needs to be set. As a result of those changes, the `beforeFirstNode` function in each rule has to changed to something like below:
201→
202→```kotlin
203→public class SomeRule : Rule(
204→  ruleId = RuleId("some-rule-set:some-rule"),
205→  usesEditorConfigProperties = setOf(MY_EDITOR_CONFIG_PROPERTY),
206→) {
207→  private lateinit var myEditorConfigProperty: MyEditorConfigProperty
208→
209→  override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
210→    myEditorConfigProperty = editorConfig[MY_EDITOR_CONFIG_PROPERTY]
211→  }

217→Fields `loadOnlyWhenOtherRuleIsLoaded` and `runOnlyWhenOtherRuleIsEnabled` have been removed from class `com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule.VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule` and are replaced with a single field `mode`. The `mode` either contains value `REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED` or `ONLY_WHEN_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_AND_ENABLED`.

219→Like before, the API Consumer can still offer a mix of rules originating from `ktlint-ruleset-standard` as well as custom rules.
220→
221→#### `.editorconfig` property `max_line_length` default value
222→
223→Previously the default value for `.editorconfig` property `max_line_length` was set to `-1` in ktlint unless the property was defined explicitly in the `.editorconfig` or when `ktlint_code_style` was set to Android. As a result of that rules have to check that max_line_length contains a positive value before checking that the actual line length is exceeding the maximum. Now the value `Int.MAX_VALUE` (use constant `MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF` to refer to that value) is used instead. 
224→
225→### Added
226→
227→* Add new experimental rule for `ktlint_official` code style that disallows a class to start with a blank line `no-empty-first-line-in-class-body`.
228→* Add new experimental rule for `ktlint_official` code style that enforces consistent usage of braces in all branches of a singe if or if-else-if statement `if-else-bracing`.
229→* Add new experimental rule for `ktlint_official` code style that disallows consecutive comments except EOL comments `no-consecutive-comments`
230→* Wrap the type or value of a function or class parameter in case the maximum line length is exceeded `parameter-wrapping` ([#1846](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1846))
231→* Wrap the type or value of a property in case the maximum line length is exceeded `property-wrapping` ([#1846](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1846))
232→
233→### Removed
234→
235→* Remove support of the `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules`. See [disabled rules](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/configuration-ktlint/#disabled-rules) for more information.
236→
237→### Fixed
238→
239→* An enumeration class having a primary constructor and in which the list of enum entries is followed by a semicolon then do not remove the semicolon in case it is followed by code element `no-semi` ([#1733](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1733))
240→* Do not add the (first line of the) body expression on the same line as the function signature in case the max line length would be exceeded. `function-signature`. 
241→* Do not add the first line of a multiline body expression on the same line as the function signature in case function body expression wrapping property is set to `multiline`. `function-signature`. 
242→* Disable the `standard:filename` rule whenever Ktlint CLI is run with option `--stdin` ([#1742](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1742))
243→* The parameters of a function literal containing a multiline parameter list are aligned with first parameter whenever the first parameter is on the same line as the start of that function literal (not allowed in `ktlint_official` code style) `indent` ([#1756](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1756))
244→* Do not throw exception when enum class does not contain entries `trailing-comma-on-declaration-site` ([#1711](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1711))
245→* Fix continuation indent for a dot qualified array access expression in `ktlint_official` code style only `indent` ([#1540](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1540))
246→* When generating the `.editorconfig` use value `off` for the `max_line_length` property instead of value `-1` to denote that lines are not restricted to a maximum length ([#1824](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1824))
247→* Do not report an "unnecessary semicolon" after adding a trailing comma to an enum class containing a code element after the last enum entry `trailing-comma-on-declaration-site` ([#1786](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1786))
248→* A newline before a function return type should not be removed in case that leads to exceeding the maximum line length `function-return-type-spacing` ([#1764](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1764))
249→
250→### Changed
251→* Wrap the parameters of a function literal containing a multiline parameter list (only in `ktlint_official` code style) `parameter-list-wrapping` ([#1681](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1681)).
252→  Changelog:
253→* KtLint CLI exits with an error in any of following cases (this list is not exhaustive):
254→  - A custom ruleset jar is to be loaded and that jar contains a deprecated RuleSetProviderV2.
255→  - A custom ruleset jar is to be loaded and that jar does not contain the required RuleSetProviderV3.
256→  - A custom reporter jar is to be loaded and that jar contains a deprecated ReporterProvider.
257→  - A custom reporter jar is to be loaded and that jar does not contain the required ReporterProviderV2.
258→* Disable the default patterns if the option `--patterns-from-stdin` is specified ([#1793](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1793))
259→* Update Kotlin development version to `1.8.20-Beta` and Kotlin version to `1.8.10`.
260→* Revert to matrix build to speed up build, especially for the Windows related build ([#1787](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1787))
261→
262→## [0.48.2] - 2023-01-21
263→
264→### Additional clarification on API Changes in `0.48.0` and `0.48.1`
265→
266→Starting with Ktlint `0.48.x`, rule and rule sets can be enabled/disabled with a separate property per rule (set). Please read [deprecation of (ktlint_)disable_rules property](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/faq/#why-is-editorconfig-property-disabled_rules-deprecated-and-how-do-i-resolve-this) for more information.
267→
268→API Consumers that provide experimental rules to the KtLintRuleEngine, must also enable the experimental rules or instruct their users to do so in the `.editorconfig` file. From the perspective of the API Consumer it might be confusing or unnecessary to do so as the experimental rule was already provided explicitly.
269→
270→Ktlint wants to provide the user (e.g. a developer) a uniform and consistent user experience. The `.editorconfig` becomes more and more central to store configuration for Ktlint. This to ensure that all team members use the exact same configuration when running ktlint regardless whether the Ktlint CLI or an API Consumer is being used.
271→
272→The `.editorconfig` is a powerful configuration tool which can be used in very different ways. Most projects use a single `.editorconfig` file containing one common section for kotlin and kotlin scripts files. For example, the `.editorconfig` file of the Ktlint project contains following section:
273→```editorconfig
274→[*.{kt,kts}]
275→ij_kotlin_imports_layout = *
276→ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma = true
277→ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma_on_call_site = true
278→```
279→Other projects might contain multiple `.editorconfig` files for different parts of the project directory hierarchy. Or, use a single `.editorconfig` file containing multiple sections with different globs. Like all other configuration settings in Ktlint, the user should be able to enable and disable the experimental rules. Both for the entire set of experimental rules and for individual experimental rules.
280→
281→Ktlint allows API Consumers to set default values and override values for the `.editorconfig`. Specifying a default value means that the user does not need to define the property in the `.editorconfig` file but if the user specifies the value, it will take precedence. Specifying the override value ensures that this takes precedence on a value specified by the user in the `.editorconfig`.
282→
283→From the Ktlint perspective, it is advised that API Consumers provide the default value. See example below, for how to specify the `editorConfigDefault` property:
284→```
285→KtLintRuleEngine(
286→    ruleProviders = ruleProviders,
287→    editorConfigDefaults = EditorConfigDefaults(
288→        EditorConfig
289→            .builder()
290→            .section(
291→                Section
292→                    .builder()
293→                    .glob(Glob("*.{kt,kts}"))
294→                    .properties(
295→                        Property
296→                            .builder()
297→                            .name("ktlint_experimental")
298→                            .value("enabled"),
299→                    ),
300→            )
301→            .build()
302→    )
303→)
304→```
305→If the user has set property `ktlint_experimental` explicitly than that value will be used. If the value is not defined, the value provided via `editorConfigDefaults` will be used.
306→
307→If you do want to ignore the value of `ktlint_experimental` as set by the user, than you can set the EditorConfigOverride property. But as said before that is discouraged as the user might not understand why the `.editorconfig` property is being ignored (provided that the value set is not equal to the value provided by the API Consumer).
308→
309→### Added
310→

312→

314→* Fix with array-syntax annotations on the same line as other annotations `annotation` ([#1765](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1765))
315→* Do not enable the experimental rules by default when `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` or `ktlint_disabled_rules` are set. ([#1771](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1771))
316→* A function signature not having any parameters which exceeds the `max-line-length` should be ignored by rule `function-signature` ([#1773](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1773))
317→* Allow diacritics in names of classes, functions packages, and properties `class-naming`, `function-naming`, `package-name`, `property-naming` ([#1757](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1757))
318→* Prevent violation of `file-name` rule on code snippets ([#1768](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1768))
319→* Clarify that API Consumers have to enable experimental rules ([#1768](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1768))
320→* Trim spaces in the `.editorconfig` property `ij_kotlin_imports_layout`'s entries ([#1770](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1770))

343→## [0.48.0] - 2022-12-15
344→
345→### Indent rule
346→
347→The `indent` rule has been rewritten from scratch. Solving problems in the old algorithm was very difficult. With the new algorithm this becomes a lot easier. Although the new implementation of the rule has been compared against several open source projects containing over 400,000 lines of code, it is still likely that new issues will be discovered. Please report your indentation issues so that these can be fixed as well.
348→
349→### `.editorconfig` property to disable rules
350→
351→In the previous release (0.47.x), the `.editorconfig`  property `disabled_rules` was deprecated and replaced with `ktlint_disabled_rules`. This latter property has now been deprecated as well in favour of a more flexible and better maintainable solution. Rule and rule sets can now be enabled/disabled with a separate property per rule (set). Please read [deprecation of (ktlint_)disable_rules property](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/faq/#why-is-editorconfig-property-disabled_rules-deprecated-and-how-do-i-resolve-this) for more information.
352→
353→The KtLint CLI has not been changed. Although you can still use parameter `--experimental` to enable KtLint's Experimental rule set, you might want to set `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental = enabled` instead.
354→
355→### API Changes & RuleSet providers
356→
357→If you are not an API consumer or Rule Set provider then you can skip this section.
358→
359→#### Class relocations
360→
361→Classes below have been relocated:
362→
363→* Class `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.UsesEditorConfigProperties.EditorConfigProperty` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfigProperty`. 
364→* Class `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.KtLintParseException` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.KtLintParseException`.
365→* Class `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.RuleExecutionException` has been replaced with `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.KtLintRuleException`.
366→* Class `com.pinterest.ktlint.reporter.format.internal.Color` has been moved to `com.pinterest.ktlint.reporter.format.Color`.
367→* Class `com.pinterest.ktlint.reporter.plain.internal.Color` has been moved to `com.pinterest.ktlint.reporter.plain.Color`.
368→
369→#### Invoking `lint` and `format`
370→
371→This is the last release that supports the `ExperimentalParams` to invoke the `lint` and `format` functions of KtLint. The `ExperimentalParams` contains a mix of configuration settings which are not dependent on the file/code which is to be processed. Other parameters in that class describe the code/file to be processed but can be configured inconsistently (for example a file with name "foo.kt" could be marked as a Kotlin Script file).
372→
373→The static object `KtLint` is deprecated and replaced by class `KtLintRuleEngine` which is configured with `KtLintRuleEngineConfiguration`. The instance of the `KtLintRuleEngine` is intended to be reused for scanning all files in a project and should not be recreated per file.
374→
375→Both `lint` and `format` are simplified and can now be called for a code block or for an entire file.
376→
377→```kotlin
378→import java.io.File
379→
380→// Define a reusable instance of the KtLint Rule Engine
381→val ktLintRuleEngine = KtLintRuleEngine(
382→  // Define configuration
383→)
384→
385→
386→// Process a collection of files
387→val files: Set<File> // Collect files in a convenient way
388→files.forEach(file in files) {
389→    ktLintRuleEngine.lint(file) {
390→        // Handle lint violations
391→    }
392→}
393→
394→// or process a code sample for a given filepath
395→ktLintRuleEngine.lint(
396→  code = "code to be linted",
397→  filePath = Path("/path/to/source/file")
398→) {
399→  // Handle lint violations

content.thinking:
**Summarizing final changes and tests run**

I'll prepare a clear summary highlighting the key changes made to AnnotationRule, including new helper functions and tests, plus the specific test command run. I'll also note limitations around formatting and mention any relevant changelog entries to give a full picture of the update.

content.text:
Summary:
- Extended `AnnotationRule` so annotated type arguments with multiple annotations are now wrapped deterministically: first annotations inside a `TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST` are forced onto their own line, sibling annotations inside the same type argument are split onto separate lines, and helpers ensure the rule only triggers when needed.
- Added dedicated unit coverage in the new `AnnotationRuleTypeArgumentListTest`, capturing both the failing scenario from #673 and the already correctly formatted case.
- Documented the fix in `CHANGELOG.md` under the “Fixed” section.

Tests:
- `./gradlew ktlint-ruleset-standard:test --tests com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules.AnnotationRuleTypeArgumentListTest`
